 Chemistry 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Rohan D Erande 
 
 
 
 
 
 Rohan D Erande 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Rohan D Erande
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. : Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rd[dot]erande[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Synthesis of Natural Products & Medicinally Active Compounds, Method Development - Lewis Acid Catalysis, Organocatalysis, Organometallic Catalysis 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1312 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr.
Rohan D. Erande earned his B.Sc. (2006) and M.Sc. (Organic Chemistry) in 2008
from Dr. Babasaheb Ambedkar Marathwada University, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar
and then joined the laboratory of Prof. Dattatraya H. Dethe at Indian Institute
of Technology Kanpur (IITK), Uttar Pradesh, where he earned his doctoral degree
(In 2015) in the field of Natural Product Synthesis. Along with the Ph.D.
degree, he was also recipient of the prestigious Eli-Lilly Asia Outstanding
Thesis Award for the year of 2015. Subsequently, he joined Professor K. C.
Nicolaou’s group as a postdoctoral fellow at Rice University, Houston, Texas,
USA (2015-2018). Furthermore, February 2019 – April 2019, he worked at
Max-Planck-Institut für Kohlenforschung, Mülheim/Ruhr, Germany, as a postdoctoral
fellow with Prof. Alois Fürstner before he offered an Assistant Professor
position from IIT Jodhpur. He began his independent career in April 2019 at
Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, Rajasthan. From January 2025, he has
been an Associate Professor at the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jodhpur, where
his current research is focused on the method development and its application
to the total synthesis of biologically active natural products. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 His research group mainly
focused on method development particularly organometallic catalysis and Lewis
acid catalysed cascade reactions to resolve the complexity of targeted natural
products to achieve the total synthesis in a short and efficient way. An aiming
to target potent drug molecules with new mechanisms of action, his group is
exploring the field of medicinal chemistry by collaboration with
bio-laboratories and pharmaceutical companies towards drug discovery and
development. 
 Research Objectives : 
 Lewis
     acid catalysis 
 Organometallic
     catalysis 
 Cascade
     reactions 
 Natural
     Product Synthesis Medicinal Chemistry

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Vivek Wakchaure 
 
 
 
 
 
 Vivek Wakchaure 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Vivek Wakchaure
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: CSIR -National Chemical Laboratory (NCL), Pune, Maharashtra 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        vivekwakchaure[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Target-Oriented Organic Synthesis; Multispin Molecular Architectures; Spin-Active Organic Materials; Two-Dimensional (2D) Polymers.
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1318 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Vivek Wakchaure is
from Parner-Ahilyanagar in Maharashtra, India. He earned his Bachelor of
Science (B.Sc.) in Chemistry from New Arts, Commerce, and Science College,
Parner ( June 2008-May 2011 ), followed by a Master of Science (M.Sc.) in
Organic Chemistry from the Dr. John Barnabas Post Graduate School for
Biological Studies, B.P.H.E. Society's Ahmednagar College, affiliated with Pune
University ( June 2011-May 2013 ). Between Aug  2013 -June 2015 , he
worked as a project assistant in Dr. C. V. Ramana’s research group at the
CSIR-National Chemical Laboratory (CSIR-NCL), Pune. In 2021, he was
awarded a Ph.D. in Chemical Science from CSIR-NCL under the supervision of Dr.
Sukumaran Santhosh Babu. His doctoral research focused on the development of
processable two-dimensional (2D) polymers using solvent-free organic liquids.
From  Jan 2021-Dec 2023 , Dr. Vivek pursued postdoctoral research in the
Bonifazi group at the University of Vienna, Austria, contributing to the
DECOCHROM project and working on boron–nitrogen (BN)-doped materials. Further
from  Jan 2024-Dec 2025 , he was postdoctoral researcher, in Delius Group
at Ulm University and contributed to the QuE-MRI and the Cluster of Excellence
POLiS (Post-Lithium Storage) projects, while also focusing on the design and
synthesis of multispin systems. He is currently an Assistant Professor at IIT
Jodhpur, pursuing research on functional organic materials for OLEDs, energy
storage, and quantum spin-based technologies, alongside teaching and mentoring
in organic chemistry 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 His current research is focused on the rational molecular design and multistep synthesis of functional organic materials, with particular emphasis on the development of chemically robust organic radicals exhibiting precisely tunable electronic, optical, and spin properties. The work systematically investigates structure property relationships through controlled modification of molecular frameworks, substituent effects, and conjugation pathways to understand spin delocalization and stabilization mechanisms in radical-based systems. Beyond discrete molecular architectures, the research extends to the chemical integration of organic radical systems with two-dimensional (2D) materials, enabling the construction of hybrid organic–2D heterostructures for spintronic applications. Comprehensive synthetic, spectroscopic, and physicochemical characterization is employed to correlate molecular structure, electronic configuration, and spin-related functionality, thereby guiding the design of next-generation radical-based functional materials. The primary research objectives are as follows: a) Synthesize stable organic radicals with tuneable electronic and optical properties. b) Establish structure property relationships and spin delocalization radical-based functional organic materials.

 People 
 
 
 Sandip Murarka 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sandip Murarka 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sandip Murarka
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: University of Muenster, Germany 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sandipmurarka[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Photoinduced Synthetic Transformations, Electro-organic synthesis, Radical Chemistry, C-H Functionalizations, Transition Metal Catalysis, Medicinal Chemistry 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1310 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Murarka pursued his PhD from WWU Münster under the supervision of Prof. Armido Studer. After completing his Ph.D. (2013), he worked as a Max-Planck postdoctoral research fellow in the laboratory of Prof. Herbert Waldmann at Max Planck Institute of Molecular Physiology, Dortmund (2013-2016). Following a year-long stay (2016-2017) as a Team Leader in a reputed pharmaceutical company, Syngene International Limited, he decided to return to academia. In May 2017, he joined the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, India, as an Assistant Professor and was promoted to the post of Associate Professor in June 2022. He is a recipient of the prestigious ‘Institute Senior Researcher Award (Science Category)’ by IIT Jodhpur, ‘The Early Career Research Award’ by SERB, Government of India, ‘The Thieme Chemistry Journal Award’ by Thieme, and ‘The Merck Young Scientist Award’ by Merck. He is an Early Career Advisory Board Member (ECAB) of several journals, such as ChemistrySelect (Wiley-VCH), Chemistry an Asian Journal (Wiley-VCH), and Organic Chemistry Frontiers (RSC). He is also a Fellow of the Indian Chemical Society (FICS) and a Member of the Royal Society of Chemistry (MRSC). 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Murarka’s current research activities include the study of novel activation modes and the development of chemoselective and sustainable synthetic transformations. His research group utilizes sustainable chemical tools, such as base-metal catalysis, photocatalysis, and electro-organic synthesis, to convert feedstock chemicals to value-added compounds and pharmaceutically relevant molecular architectures. The thrust areas of research in his group include: Radical Chemistry Photoinduced Synthetic Transformations Electrochemistry in Organic Synthesis Transition Metal Catalyzed Cross-Couplings C-H Functionalizations Medicinal Chemistry

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Koushambi Mitra 
 
 
 
 
 
 Koushambi Mitra 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Koushambi Mitra
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD: Indian Institute of Science 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        kmitra[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Chemical Biology: organic synthesis, quantitative reporters, cell and organism imaging, organelle biology 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1349 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Koushambi Mitra obtained her bachelor's degree in chemistry from Presidency College, University of Calcutta (2006-2009). Mitra then joined as an integrated MS-PhD student at the Indian Institute of Science and developed platinum complexes as light activated DNA crosslinking and anticancer agents (2009-2016). Thereafter, Mitra did a series of brief postdoctoral stints (Virginia Commonwealth University, 2016-2018; and the University of Texas at Dallas, 2018-2020) where she switched her research interests to organic synthesis and chemical biology. She developed unique photocages for cancer drug delivery and activity-based sensors for detection of enzymatic activity in living neuronal cells. In her last postdoc at the University of Chicago (2020-2024), she developed ratiometric sensors for quantitative imaging of organellar sodium ions. Currently, Mitra is an assistant professor in the department of chemistry at IIT Jodhpur (July, 2024) where her research interests lie in the highly interdisciplinary areas of chemical biology. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 We are developing cutting-edge chemical technologies to understand the biochemical and chemical environment of organelles. Our main approach is to enable the chemistry done in reaction vessels to occur in a much complex cellular milieu. Though substantial advances have been made to understand the cell as a whole entity, we still lack probes and methods to study biological processes specifically in organelles in living system as a function of time. To meet this dire need, we are designing and developing chemical platforms which will allow us to map and correlate organelles based on morphological and functional properties. The ongoing projects of our research group are: 1. Developing organelle-targeted fluorescent probes for physiological ions and metabolites and study them in the context of health and diseases 2. Developing activity-based chemical probes for detecting enzyme activities of mitochondrial oxidative phosphorylation (OXPHOS) and reductive pathways 3. Developing biorthogonal and bioconjugation reactions for specific tagging of metabolites or proteins in the organelles

 Chemistry 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Dibyendu Kumar Sasmal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Dibyendu Kumar Sasmal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Dibyendu Kumar Sasmal
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sasmal[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Experimental Physical Chemistry, Fluorescence Spectroscopy and Imaging, Biophysical Chemistry
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1314 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Dibyendu K. Sasmal is an Associate Professor in
the Department of Chemistry at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur,
where he has been serving since July 2023, following his tenure as an Assistant
Professor from 2019 to 2023. His academic journey began with a Bachelor of
Science (Honors) in Chemistry from Medinipur College, Vidyasagar University, in
2005, followed by a Master of Science (M.Sc.) in Chemistry with a
specialization in Physical Chemistry in 2007. He earned his Ph.D. in Physical
Chemistry in 2012 from the Indian Association for the Cultivation of Science,
Kolkata, under the guidance of Professor Kankan Bhattacharyya. Post-Ph.D., he
conducted postdoctoral research at Bowling Green State University, Ohio, USA,
with Professor H. Peter Lu, followed by research at the Pritzker School of
Molecular Engineering, University of Chicago, under Professor Jun Huang, and
later at the Center for Nanoscale Materials, Argonne National Laboratory,
Illinois, with Professor Tijana Rajh. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sasmal and his group is interested to
understand complex interplay among structure, function, and dynamics of bio-molecules
present either in condensed phase or cellular environment at single molecular
level in real-time. Conventional ensemble methods can only give information
related to molecular function which are average of property with respect to
time. However, single-molecule spectroscopy has an unique capabilities for distinguishing
molecular properties with spatiotemporal resolution of sub-nano meter and pico-seconds.
Their approach is highly interdisciplinary combining molecular spectroscopy, molecular
biology, optics and advanced fluorescence microscopy. The group is currently
investigating the pattern of conformational dynamics of DNA, protein and RNA. They
are also trying to understand how immune cells distinguish between self and
foreign antigens at single molecule level in real time. Drug-protein
interaction in condensed phase, and development of quantum dot based drug carrier,
fluorescence correlation spectroscopy (FCS) and fluorescence lifetime imaging
microscopy (FLIM) are also topic of research for this group.

 Chemistry 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Atul Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Atul Kumar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Atul Kumar
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        atulk[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Quantum Information Processing 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1304 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Atul Kumar received his B.Sc.
degree in Chemistry from the Department of Chemistry, Banaras Hindu University,
in 1999, and his M.Sc. degree in Chemistry from the Department of Chemistry,
Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Madras, in 2004, with a focus on
theoretical chemistry. He completed his Ph.D. in 2010 at the Department of
Chemistry, IIT Madras (IITM), specializing in Quantum Information and
Computation. He joined IIT Jodhpur in March 2012, where he is currently a
Professor in the Department of Chemistry. His research group is interested in
exploring the frontiers of quantum computing in the noisy intermediate-scale
quantum (NISQ) era, focusing on algorithm development, quantum entanglement,
and strategies to manage noise and errors. A key aspect of our work involves
leveraging quantum methods to tackle challenges in computational chemistry and
open quantum dynamics. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Over the past three decades,
quantum technology has garnered significant public and scientific attention,
driven by sustained efforts from both academia and industry to bring
quantum information and computation to the forefront of technological advancement.
The paradigm shift from classical to quantum computing holds the promise of
solving classically intractable problems more efficiently. However, the
presence of quantum noise and limitations in circuit size—characteristic of the
current noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) era—pose substantial challenges
to realizing the full potential of fault-tolerant quantum computing. 
 Our research group is
actively engaged in addressing several key challenges in quantum information
and computation within the constraints of the NISQ era. Our interests include
computational chemistry in the realm of quantum computation, the classification
and quantification of multiqubit entanglement and nonlocality, the development
of efficient quantum algorithms for a range of applications, error mitigation
strategies, and the study of open quantum systems.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Shyam Kumar Banjare 
 
 
 
 
 
 Shyam Kumar Banjare 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Shyam Kumar Banjare
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: National Institute of Science Education and Research (NISER), Bhubaneswar 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        shyambanjare[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Research Interests in Transition Metal Catalysis, N-Heterocyclic Carbene Chemistry, and Organic Synthesis 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1319 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Shyam Kumar Banjare earned his Bachelor of Science degree from Government M.M.R.P.G. College, Champa, and his Master of Science degree in Chemistry from Government Autonomous Science College Bilaspur, both affiliated with Bilaspur University, in 2013 and 2015, respectively. After completing his master’s degree, he qualified for the CSIR–NET–LS. He subsequently served as a temporary faculty member in the Department of Chemistry at Guru Ghasidas Vishwavidyalaya (a Central University), Bilaspur, from 2016 to 2017. He later joined the National Institute of Science Education and Research (NISER), Bhubaneswar, as a Junior Research Fellow under the supervision of Prof. Ponneri C. Ravikumar, where he completed his doctoral degree in March 2023. His PhD research focused on cobalt-catalyzed C–H bond functionalization. Following his doctoral studies, he joined the University of Münster, Germany, as a postdoctoral researcher with Prof. Armido Studer, where his research centered on radical reactions and photoredox chemistry. He is currently serving as an Assistant Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, with research interests in transition metal catalysis, N-heterocyclic carbene chemistry and organic synthesis. 
 
 
 
 
                        Academic Award
                     
 
 
 Recipient of the Outstanding Doctoral Student Award (Best Thesis) from the Homi Bhabha National Institute HBNI, Mumbai, India, in 2024. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 His research program focuses on the design and development of innovative and broadly applicable catalytic platforms for contemporary organic synthesis. We harness the unique reactivity of N-heterocyclic carbenes (NHCs), both as privileged ligands in transition-metal catalysis and as powerful organocatalysts, to enable challenging C–C, C–N, and C–X bond-forming and bond-activation processes. By synergistically integrating transition-metal catalysis with NHC catalysis and Lewis acid activation, our approach enables precise molecular construction, exceptional efficiency, and high levels of chemo-, regio-, and stereoselectivity, while uncovering fundamentally new reaction manifolds. This research program offers an intellectually stimulating environment for students to engage in state-of-the-art catalysis, gain deep mechanistic understanding, and make creative, impactful contributions toward advancing the frontiers of catalytic science and synthetic methodology. Research Objectives: Develop innovative NHC-based catalytic systems for modern organic synthesis. Integrate transition-metal catalysis with NHCs to achieve synergistic reactivity Train students in state-of-the-art catalytic methods and mechanistic analysis. Discover new reaction manifolds and gain fundamental mechanistic insights in catalysis.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Nirmal Kumar Rana 
 
 
 
 
 
 Nirmal Kumar Rana 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Nirmal Kumar Rana
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        nirmalrana[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Asymmetric Synthesis & Continuous Flow Chemistry 
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1311 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Rana obtained his Ph.D. from the
Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur (IITK), India, in 2012 under the supervision
of Prof. Vinod K. Singh. Then, he moved to the research group of Prof. John
C.-G. Zhao at the University of Texas at San Antonio (UTSA), USA. After two
years post-doctoral research at UTSA, he returned to India, and worked as a
senior research scientist in the Department of Chemistry, IITK for one year.
Then, in 2015, he moved to the Indian Institute of Science Education and
Research Bhopal as an INSPIRE Faculty Fellow. In November 2017, he joined as a
faculty in the Department of Chemistry, Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur,
India. He is currently serving as an Associate Professor in the Department of
Chemistry, IIT Jodhpur. He is recipient of many prestigious awards and
recognitions, such as, First Prize of Eli Lilly Thesis Awards in 2012, DST-INSPIRE
Faculty Award in 2015. He has recently been awarded with Chirantan Rasayan
Sanstha (CRS) Young Scientist Medal in 2024. He was a Review Editor of the
Editorial Board of Catalytic Reactions and Chemistry from 2021 to 2024. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 His research interest includes to
design and synthesize new organocatalysts and apply them to develop novel
asymmetric methodologies, mainly emphasizing domino/cascade/sequential
reactions to create multiple stereocenters within a molecule. Additional
targets are the exploration of dual organo-metal catalysis and bio-catalysis. His
research group has been actively working on the development of novel
methodologies using continuous-flow systems with immobilized catalysts for
producing fine chemicals and chiral drug molecules/intermediates with
industrial implementation. The developed protocols are utilized as key steps
for the synthesis of architecturally interesting and biologically active
molecules.

 People 
 
 
 Samanwita Pal 
 
 
 
 
 
 Samanwita Pal 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Samanwita Pal
                         
 
                                Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        samanwita[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Solution and solid state NMR and NQR spectroscopy
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1305 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Dr. Samanwita Pal is an NMR
spectroscopist and a Professor at the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jodhpur. She
earned a BSc. (Hons.) from Jadavpur University and a MSc degree in Chemistry
from IIT Madras. She further received her PhD in small molecule NMR and NQR
spectroscopy from IIT Madras in 2010 under the supervision of Prof. N.
Chandrakumar. She pursued her postdoctoral fellowship in the group of Prof.
Stephan Grzesiek at Biozentrum, University of Basel, Switzerland during
2010-2012. She joined the Department of Chemistry, IIT Jodhpur in 2012 December
as an Assistant Professor. Currently she is continuing as a Professor at the
Department of Chemistry.  
 Dr. Pal received Ratna Rao
Memorial prize and Dr. S. R. Ramadas 60 th  Birthday Commemoration
award for best academic record in Chemistry during the period 2002-2004. She earned
the prestigious Prof Langmuir Prize for best PhD Thesis in Physical and
Theoretical Chemistry from IIT Madras in 2010. She has served as an elected
Executive Committee member of the National Magnetic Resonance Society, India
from March 2021 for a period of three years. She has published several articles
in reputed journals and presented her findings in numerous national and
international meetings as an invited speaker. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 Dr. Pal is working in small
molecule solution-state and solid-state NMR and NQR for past two decades. Her
research interest at present focusses on design and application of NMR methods
to decipher molecular dynamics and interactions relevant in a variety of areas
ranging from energy, environment to defence and medicine. Her research group
utilizes the 500 MHz WB NMR spectrometer from the central facility and a 20 MHz
Time Domain (TD) NMR spectrometer housed in her research laboratory. The
research group majorly implements NMR relaxation, diffusion and magnetization
transfer methods. Some of the current thrust areas the group is working in are
as follows: 
 NMR
relaxometry, diffusometry and Cryoporometry  Small molecule
solution state magnetization transfer and correlation experiments NMR Solid
state ion dynamics in porous materials NMR chemical
exchange in condensed phase NQR detection
and instrumentation Pulse sequence design and modification

 Chemistry 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Suresh Sarkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Suresh Sarkar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Suresh Sarkar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D.: Indian association for the cultivation of Science 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sarkar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Nanomaterials; NIR-Quantum dots; Spectroscopy; Surface Chemistry; Shape control; Photo-catalysis; photo-voltaics; cell-imaging
 
 
 
 
 call 
 
  0291 280 1346 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        Biography
                     
 
 
 Suresh
Sarkar is a dedicated researcher specializing in the synthesis and application
of nanomaterials. He earned his PhD in 2014 from the Indian Association for the
Cultivation of Science (IACS), Kolkata, under the mentorship of Professor
Narayan Pradhan, where he made significant contributions to the field through
extensive publications. Following his doctoral studies, he pursued postdoctoral
research at Vanderbilt University, USA (2014–2016), further deepening his
expertise in nanomaterials. He then undertook a second postdoctoral fellowship
at the University of Illinois at Urbana-Champaign, USA, under Professor Andrew
M. Smith, completing it in 2022. Currently, Dr. Sarkar serves as an Assistant
Professor at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. His research
focuses on the development of advanced functional nanomaterials with
applications in photovoltaics, catalysis, and related energy. His work aims to
bridge the gap between fundamental nanoscience and real-world technological
advancements, contributing to the next generation of high-performance
materials. 
 
 
 
 
                        Research
                     
 
 
 His
research focuses on development of novel functional semiconductor nanomaterials
and understand their crystal growth and photo-physical behaviour
in solution. Surface
engineering of quantum dots:  Quantum
dots are well-known for their quantum confinement and high surface to volume
ratio. The surface structure/morphology of quantum dots plays pivotal role in
controlling their photo-physical properties and applications in nanotechnology.
Our research group focuses on surface engineering of quantum dots for achieving
novel functional properties that can enhance their efficiency in
nanotechnological applications. Transition
Metal Ion Doping  :
Doping semiconductor nanocrystals with transition metal ions unlocks unique
properties for optoelectronics. Our research explores optimal host-guest
combinations to develop novel functional nanomaterials. Anisotropic Semiconductor Nanomaterials  :
They offer enhanced electron transport, making them ideal for optoelectronics,
sensors, and photocatalysis. Our research focuses on designing tunable
semiconductor nanocrystals for advanced catalytic applications. Heterostructure Nanomaterials: 
Integrating multiple components in a nanocrystal creates unique properties for
applications in photovoltaics and photocatalysis. Our research focuses on
synthesizing heterostructured
nanomaterials and exploring their functional potential. Photovoltaics:  Our
research explores semiconductor nanomaterials for efficient solar energy
conversion. We assess their light absorption potential and develop low-cost
photodetectors to evaluate their suitability for next-generation solar cells. Photocatalysis: 
We develop near-infrared absorbing semiconductor nanocrystals for sustainable
energy and organic synthesis. Our focus is on utilizing them in single-step
reactions to produce commercially valuable compounds.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sanhita Das 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sanhita Das 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sanhita Das
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Science Bangalore 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sanhitadas[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Computational Mechanics; Constitutive Modelling; Smart Materials; Amorphous Materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1666 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sanhita Das  is an  Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur . Her research focuses on  computational mechanics for understanding the constitutive behavior of amorphous solids, damage and self-healing in polymers, concrete, and geomaterials, as well as the development of smart materials with stimulus sensitivity . She is also interested in  smart deployable structures for energy harvesting and post-disaster management , with a future research focus on  Physics-Informed Neural Networks . Dr. Das holds a  Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from the Indian Institute of Science (2022), an M.Tech. in Structural Engineering from IIT Kharagpur (2013), and a B.Tech. in Civil Engineering from Jadavpur University (2010) . Before joining IIT Jodhpur, she gained diverse industry and research experience. She worked as a  Civil Engineer at M.N. Dastur & Company (2010-2011)  and as an  Engineer in the Structure and Thermal Analysis Department at Airbus Group India Pvt. Ltd. (2013-2016) . She also held academic research positions as a  Research Associate at IISc Bangalore (2021-2022)  and a  Postdoctoral Fellow at the Weizmann Institute of Science, Israel (2022-2023) . 
 At IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Das is involved in teaching  Engineering Mechanics, Cyber-Physical Systems in Civil Engineering, Mechanics of Solids, Smart Infrastructure Engineering, and the Finite Element Method . Passionate about material behavior and computational mechanics, she invites students and researchers to explore the fascinating world of materials and structural modeling through her research.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Trishikhi Raychoudhury 
 
 
 
 
 
 Trishikhi Raychoudhury 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Trishikhi Raychoudhury
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. McGill University, Canada 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        trishikhi[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Environmental Engineering: colloid filtration; contaminant fate and transport; water treatment; applications and implications of nanotechnology 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1663 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Raychoudhury,  an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. She has a wide range of experience in water quality, remediation, and management related issues, starting from laboratory experiment to numerical modelling to real-field scenario. Prior to joining to IIT Jodhpur she was working as an Assistant Professor (2015-2021) in IIT Patna and BITS-Pilani Hyderabad (2014-2015).  After her M.Tech. (2005) from IIT Kanpur, she joined International Water Management Institute (IWMI)-Tata, as a Research Consultant (2005-2007). During her stay at IWMI-Tata she got wide exposure about real-field scenario on water quality related issues. Her research during Ph.D. (2011) at McGill University, Canada, and PostDoc at Technische Universitat Berlin (2012-2014), Germany was mainly focused on the application of advanced nanoparticle-based techniques for water quality control. Her research motivation is towards linking the real-field water contamination issues with the laboratory based advanced solution. Furthermore, she believes that it is equally important to investigate the possible environmental implication of any novel technology before adopting it. She leads the group in the same direction towards achieving a more sustainable solution for the water quality sector.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Subham Jain 
 
 
 
 
 
 Subham Jain 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Subham Jain
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D: Indian Institute of Technology, Kanpur 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        sjain[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Pavement functional evaluation, Aggregate shape characterization 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1673 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Subham Jain  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. He joined the institute in September 2025. Dr. Jain completed his joint M.Tech-Ph.D. at the Indian Institute of Technology Kanpur, specializing in pavement surface texture characterization. He completed his B.Tech from NIT Silchar. Dr. Jain’s research interests lie in the field of pavement functional evaluation and aggregate shape characterization, with a particular focus on automated pavement surface texture measurement, surface texture analysis, pavement friction estimation from surface texture and automated aggregate angularity estimation. He has published various peer-reviewed publications and conference papers, and has patents granted in these research areas. He has also received the May award for engineering excellence by IIT Kanpur and the iGrip research award for Master’s thesis by IIT Gandhinagar.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Saran Aadhar 
 
 
 
 
 
 Saran Aadhar 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Saran Aadhar
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        saran[dot]aadhar[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Surface Hydrology; Hydroclimatic extremes; Hydrologic modeling in natural and anthropogenic climate; Impact of climate variability and climate change on water resources 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1662 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Saran Aadhar  is an Assistant Professor in the  Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur  and is affiliated with the  Center for Intelligent Infrastructure, School of AI and Data Sciences . His research focuses on  real-time monitoring of drought and floods, sub-seasonal to seasonal forecasting of hydrological extremes, hydrological processes under changing and anthropogenic climates, and the impact of climate change on water resources . Dr. Aadhar earned his Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from the  Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar  in 2021 and completed his B.Tech. in Civil Engineering from the  National Institute of Technology Raipur  in 2015. As part of his academic journey, he was a  Visiting Scholar at the University of Nebraska-Lincoln, USA  in 2019. Before joining IIT Jodhpur, he worked as a  Postdoctoral Researcher at the Hebrew University of Jerusalem, Israel , from December 2020 to November 2021. Recognized for his contributions to hydrology and climate science, Dr. Aadhar has received numerous prestigious awards and honors. He is a  Young Associate of the Indian Academy of Sciences  and a recipient of the  2024 CDRI Fellowship . His research excellence has been acknowledged with the  Best Discussion Award (2022) by the Journal of Hydrological Engineering (ASCE)  and the  Sivapalan Young Scientists Travel Award from the International Association of Hydrological Sciences (IAHS) . Additionally, he has been awarded the  Water Advanced Research and Innovation (WARI) Fellowship by the Indo-U.S. Science and Technology Forum , the  Ministry of Education Ph.D. Fellowship (2015-2020), and travel grants from SERB, SPARC, and ITRA . He has also received the  IIT Gandhinagar Cash Research Award  for his outstanding academic contributions. 
 With a strong foundation in hydrological modeling and climate impact assessment, Dr. Aadhar continues to contribute to cutting-edge research aimed at improving water resource management and mitigating the risks of extreme hydrological events.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Bhupendra Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Bhupendra Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Bhupendra Singh
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        bhupendra[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Pavement Materials; Pavement Analysis; Pavement Design; Sustainable Pavement Materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1659 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Bhupendra Singh  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on pavement materials, pavement analysis, and design. He has been serving at IIT Jodhpur since January 2021. Prior to this, he worked as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil Engineering at the National Institute of Technology Patna from July 2018 to January 2021. He also held a contractual Assistant Professor position at Dr. B.R. Ambedkar National Institute of Technology Jalandhar from January 2018 to June 2018. Dr. Singh was honored with the  Young Scientist in Transportation Systems Award  at the 5th Annual Research Meet (ARM) 2019 by Venus International Foundation. He also received the  Science and Engineering Research Board International Research Experience (SIRE) Fellowship  for 2023-24 to conduct research at the University of Nottingham. 
 His recent publications have appeared in leading journals, including the  Journal of Materials in Civil Engineering, International Journal of Pavement Engineering, Road Materials and Pavement Design, Journal of Cleaner Production, Materials and Structures,  and  Construction and Building Materials.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Aali Pant 
 
 
 
 
 
 Aali Pant 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Aali Pant
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        aalipant[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Geotechnical Engineering, Application of Geosynthetics 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1664 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Aali Pant  is an  Assistant Professor in Geotechnical Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur . Her research focuses on  geoenvironmental engineering, reinforced soil structures, geosynthetics applications, and AI/ML in geotechnical engineering . She holds a  Ph.D. from IIT Delhi (2020), an M.Tech. from IIT Roorkee (2013), and a B.Tech. from G.B.P.U.A & T, Pantnagar (2011) . Before joining IIT Jodhpur in 2022, she worked as an  Assistant Professor at Netaji Subhas University of Technology, Delhi , a  Research Associate at IIT Delhi , and a  Geotechnical Engineer at Cengrs Geotechnica . She has also contributed to major consultancy projects on Ash Dyke feasibility and design for NTPC. Dr. Pant has received several awards, including the  Tara Chand Kanti Devi Prize (IIT Roorkee), ISSMGE Foundation Award, Research Excellence Travel Award (IIT Delhi), and the Sumant Sinha-Sustainability Leadership Award . She was also  featured among the 75 Influential Indian Women in Geotechnical Engineering (2023) . 
 She is a member of  IGS, ISSMGE, and ASCE  and teaches courses on  Geotechnical Engineering, AI/ML in Civil Engineering, and Ground Improvement Techniques . Her research contributes to  sustainable geotechnical solutions and emerging technologies in civil engineering .

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Deepak Swami 
 
 
 
 
 
 Deepak Swami 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Deepak Swami
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D Indian Institute of Technology, Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        dswami[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Water resources, groundwater flow and contaminant transport modeling, One Health in agriculture, dynamic plant uptake, and parameter estimation 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1669 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Deepak Swami  is an Associate Professor in the   Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering,  Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur.  Educational Background: Dr. Swami completed his Ph.D. at the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, specializing in groundwater flow and contamination modeling, surface moisture dynamics, and geoenvironmental engineering. Professional Experience: 
 
 Associate Professor at IIT Mandi (February 2015 - March 2025):   Dr. Swami served as an Associate Professor at IIT Mandi for over a decade, contributing significantly to the field of water resources engineering.  ​ 
 
 
 Associate Professor at IIT Jodhpur (March 2025 - Present):   In March 2025, Dr. Swami joined IIT Jodhpur as an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering, bringing his extensive experience in water resources engineering to the institution.   
 
 Research Interests and Contributions: Dr. Swami is a water resources engineer and he focuses on process level investigation of groundwater flow and contaminant transport through aquifers. His research interests include parameter optimization, aquifer scale modeling, dynamic plant uptake, source identification and application of one health. . Academic Contributions: Throughout his academic career, Dr. Swami has been actively involved in research and consultancy projects, contributing to the advancement of knowledge in his field.   His expertise has been instrumental in shaping the curriculum and research initiatives at both IIT Mandi and IIT Jodhpur. ​ Professional Engagements: Dr. Swami has organized and participated in various workshops and conferences.   Notably, he was involved in the HYDRUS Winter School held from January 27-31, 2024, aimed at educating participants on the use of HYDRUS software for research and consultancy purposes. 
 Dr. Swami's transition to IIT Jodhpur marks a significant step in his career, where he continues to contribute to the field of civil and infrastructure engineering through his teaching, research, and professional activities.

 Shuvajit Mukherjee

 biotech 
 
 Metamaterials; Wave propagation; Computational mechanics; Sandwich composites 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1671 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Shuvajit Mukherjee   commenced
his tenure as an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and
Infrastructure Engineering at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur 
in February 2024. His research group is dedicated to exploring the fields of  metamaterials , 
wave propagation ,  computational mechanics ,   and  sandwich
composites . He
holds a Ph.D. from the  Indian Institute of Science , Bengaluru (2018),
an M.E. in Civil engineering (specialization: Structural Engineering) from  Jadavpur
University  (2012), and a B.E. in Civil Engineering from  Jadavpur
University  (2010). 
 
 
 
 
                        Research Experience:
                     
 
 
 Postdoc:  Technical University of Munich , Germany (2023- 2024) Postdoc:  Swansea University , Wales, UK (2020 - 2022) Postdoc:  Bauhaus University of Weimar , Germany (Bauhaus Fellow 2019-2020) Postdoc:  Indian Institute of Science , Bengaluru, India (2018 - 2019)   
 
 
 
 
                        Professional Experience:
                     
 
 
 Invited talk:             IGSTC  (Indo-German Science and Technology Center) workshop on " Vibro-acoustic metamaterial ", Civil Engineering Department, IIT Delhi, India, September, 2024           SPARC  (Scheme for Promotion of Academic and Research Collaboration) Workshop on  “Wave propagation through Metamaterial”,  Civil Engineering Department, IIT Delhi, India, March, 2024 Visiting Faculty:           Chair of Vibro-Acoustics of Vehicles and Machines, Technical University of Munich, Germany, June-July, 2024

 People 
 
 
 Akshay Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 Akshay Gupta 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Akshay Gupta
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. : Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        akshaygupta[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Traffic Engineering, Human Factors, Driving Behaviour, Road Safety, ITS and Transit Accessibility 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1672 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Akshay Gupta is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. He joined the institute in July 2025. Educational Background: Dr. Gupta completed his Ph.D. at the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee, specializing in Enhancing High-Speed Road Safety: Insights from Advanced LiDAR-Based Driver Behavior Analysis. Research Interest Dr. Gupta’s research interests lie in the field of transportation engineering, with a particular focus on road safety, driving behavior analysis, crash-risk prediction, and the application of emerging technologies such as LiDAR and camera-based sensing systems. His work contributes significantly to data-driven approaches in support of Vision Zero initiatives, aimed at eliminating traffic fatalities and ensuring safer, more sustainable mobility systems. Dr. Gupta is actively engaged in interdisciplinary research and continues to collaborate with academic and industry partners to develop innovative solutions for urban transportation challenges. Academic Contribution Over the course of his academic career, Dr. Gupta has authored over 22 peer-reviewed publications and conference papers, with his research being cited more than 1000 times. He currently holds an h-index of 13, reflecting the impact and quality of his scholarly contributions. Patents     Gupta A., Shreyansh, Priyanshu Pansari, Choudhary P, Parida M Dynamic severity-level real-time lane departure warning system with sparse 3D-Lidar sensor. (Patent published) Awards and Scholarships BEST PAPER AWARD World Conference of Transport Research (WCTR)-15    26th to 30th May, 2019     (Best Paper Award by Cairo University For Transport in Developing Countries)     Gupta, A., Bivina, G.R. and Parida, M. “Measuring Impact of Objective and Subjective Built Environment Factors on Access Mode Choice to Metro Stations.” International Conference World Conference on Transport Research (WCTR)- 15 held at IIT Bombay. Best Paper Award 2023 by the journal ‘Multimodal Transportation’     Chauhan V., Gupta, A. and Parida, M. Do users' characteristics really influence the perceived service quality of Multimodal Transportation Hub (MMTH)? An association rules mining approach" Multimodal Transportation Journal Award 2023 Outstanding Poster Award at Institute Research Day 2024 (IIT Roorkee)     Gupta, A., Choudhary P., Parida, M., Enhancing High-Speed Road Safety: Insights from Advanced LiDAR-Based Driver Behaviour Analysis.  Professional Memberships World Conference of Transport Research Society (WCTRs) Eastern Asia Society of Transportation Studies (EASTs) International Conference on Travel Behavior Research (IATBR)

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Ranju Mohan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Ranju Mohan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Ranju Mohan
                         
 
                                Associate Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ranju[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Traffic flow theory; Macroscopic and Microscopic modelling of traffic flow; Connected and autonomous vehicles; Dynamic Traffic Assignment 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1657 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Ranju Mohan  is an Associate Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. She received her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Madras and worked as a Postdoctoral Fellow at CiSTUP, Indian Institute of Science Bangalore. Her primary research interests include heterogeneous traffic flow theory and modeling, modeling connected and autonomous vehicle interactions, and dynamic traffic assignment in a multi-vehicle class scenario. 
 
 
 
 
                        Transportation System and Traffic Modelling (TSTM) Lab
                     
 
 
 TSTM lab addresses Transportation system necessities and requirements that evolve from  urban sprawl, technological advancement, and changing societal demand.  The lab primarily focuses on traffic flow modelling and simulation, accident analysis and prevention, public transport prioritization, transportation policy analysis,  and traffic management solutions. Visit the lab here  TSTM LAB 
 
 
 
 
                        Research positions open
                     
 
 
 Research positions are available in the field of Mixed traffic flow modelling, Autonomous driving, Multi-modal integrated transport, and Vehicle trajectory analysis for traffic safety and flow modelling. Email your CV to  ranju@iitj.ac.in

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sarath Chandra Reddy Nallala 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sarath Chandra Reddy Nallala 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sarath Chandra Reddy Nallala
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD (Geotechnical Engineering) : City University of Hong Kong 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        scnallala[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Multiscale mechanics of granular materials with emphasis on experimental micromechanics, discrete element modelling, laboratory validation of particle-scale simulations, and tribological behavior of geomaterials, with applications in geotechnical and geological engineering. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1675 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sarath Chandra Reddy Nallala is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure
Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, India. His research interests span the
multiscale mechanics of granular materials, experimental micromechanics, tribology, and
geotechnical and geological engineering, with a focus on bridging microscale physics and macroscale
engineering behavior.
 He earned his PhD in Geotechnical Engineering from the City University of Hong Kong, where he was
awarded the prestigious Ringo Yu Prize for the Best PhD Thesis by the Geotechnical Division of the
Hong Kong Institution of Engineers (HKIE). He completed his MTech in Geotechnical Engineering at
the Indian Institute of Technology Bombay.
 Dr. Nallala was a recipient of the CNRS Math-Vives Postdoctoral Fellowship, under which he
conducted research on the Complex Flows project at the Institut de Physique du Globe de Paris (IPGP)
and the Laboratoire de Mécanique et Génie Civil de Montpellier (LMGC), France. He actively
collaborates with researchers from Hong Kong and France, fostering international research partnerships
in experimental and computational granular mechanics.
 Complementing his academic achievements, he brings substantial industrial experience from offshore
and onshore oil and gas projects in Mumbai High and Gujarat.
 Research group
 Multiscale Assessment of Granular Materials and Assemblies (MAGMA)
 The MAGMA Group is an interdisciplinary research group focused on the multiscale analysis of
granular materials and their assemblies, combining experimental, theoretical, and computational
approaches. The group aims to develop both fundamental understanding and realistic predictive
models of granular systems, with applications in powder technology, soil mechanics, geomaterials,
construction materials, and environmental processes. 
 Research Positions available
 Several research positions are available for prospective PhD scholars and research interns in the
MAGMA Group. Motivated students with backgrounds in Civil Engineering, Mechanical Engineering,
Physics, or related disciplines are encouraged to apply. Interested applicants may send an email
outlining their research interests and motivation, along with a CV (maximum 2 pages).

 People 
 
 
 Amit Kumar Rathi 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amit Kumar Rathi 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amit Kumar Rathi
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. (Structural Engineering): Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        akrathi[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Structural Engineering; RCC Design; Steel Structures and Pre-Engineered Buildings; Reliability Analysis and Design; Uncertainty Quantification; Stochastic Modelling and Mechanics; Composite Materials; Vibration Control and Structural Health Monitoring 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1656 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Amit Kumar Rathi  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur, Rajasthan, India. His research focuses on structural engineering and applied mechanics, with a particular interest in reliability analysis and design, uncertainty quantification, stochastic modeling and mechanics, laminated composite materials, and vibration control. He earned his Bachelor's degree in Civil Engineering from the Faculty of Engineering, Jai Narain Vyas University, India. He then completed his Master’s degree in Civil Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati. In 2019, he received his Ph.D. in Structural Engineering from IIT Guwahati. 
 Prior to joining IIT Jodhpur, he was affiliated with the National Institute of Technology Sikkim and the National Institute of Technology Calicut. His research continues to contribute to advancements in structural engineering and mechanics.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Amit Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 Amit Sharma 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Amit Sharma
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
  Ph.D.(Environmental Engineering): Indian Institute of Technology Madras 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        amit[dot]sharma[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Atmospheric Chemistry; Air Pollution; Climate change; Impact on crops and human health 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1658 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Amit Sharma  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His specialization lies in environmental engineering, with a specific focus on air pollution. His research involves weather forecasting, climate modeling, and air pollution studies using complex model simulations on supercomputers. This work is supplemented by comprehensive data analysis from satellites, radiosondes, and various ground-based instruments. He has been serving as an Assistant Professor at IIT Jodhpur since July 2020. Prior to this, he worked as a Postdoctoral Research Associate at Washington State University, where he focused on wildfire modeling. He has also held visiting scholar positions at the University of Manchester, specializing in aerosol chemical speciation, and at the Max Planck Institute for Chemistry, where he contributed to weather research and forecasting. 
 Dr. Sharma’s expertise and research continue to contribute to advancements in environmental engineering and air pollution studies.

 People 
 
 
 Debanjan Guha Roy 
 
 
 
 
 
 Debanjan Guha Roy 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Debanjan Guha Roy
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Dual-badged PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Bombay and Monash University 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        dguharoy[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Rock Mechanics; Engineering Geology; Energy Geomechanics; Landslide. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1653 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Debanjan Guha  Roy is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on advancing critical areas in energy and infrastructure, contributing to sustainable and resilient development. His expertise includes geological energy storage and production, emergent geo-energy solutions, and geological engineering for infrastructure development and protection. Over the past five years, Dr. Roy’s research group has published extensively in highly respected journals, including the  International Journal of Hydrogen Energy, Microporous and Mesoporous Materials, Geomechanics for Energy and the Environment, Measurement,  and the  Journal of Rock Mechanics and Geotechnical Engineering. 
 His work continues to drive progress in geo-energy and infrastructure engineering, shaping innovative solutions for the future.

 biotech 
 
 Water and wastewater treatment; Monitoring and removal of emerging contaminants; Bioremediation; Biotransformation of trace organic pollutants; Risk assessment for water reuse 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1668 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Monika Dubey is an Assistant Professor in the
Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of
Technology (IIT) Jodhpur. Educational Background 
Dr. Dubey received her Ph.D. from the Indian Institute of Technology Roorkee,
specializing in wastewater treatment, the fate of organic micropollutants, and
environmental risk assessment. 
 
 Research Interests 
Dr. Dubey’s research expertise lies in environmental monitoring, wastewater
treatment, environmental remediation, and mass spectrometry techniques such as
UHPLC-MS/MS, with a particular focus on emerging contaminants in the
environment and their fate during wastewater treatment. She actively engages in
collaborative and interdisciplinary research to develop innovative and
sustainable solutions, contributing to advancements in sustainable wastewater
management and environmental protection. 
 Sustainable Environmental Systems (SES) Lab 
The Sustainable Environmental Systems (SES) Lab at IIT Jodhpur is dedicated to
developing innovative and sustainable technologies for environmental protection
and resource recovery. The research spans wastewater treatment, nature-based
solutions, the fate and removal of emerging contaminants, bioelectrochemical
processes, and carbon sequestration strategies. By integrating advanced
analytical tools and AI-driven approaches, SES Lab aims to address critical
challenges in water, environment, and climate sustainability. 
 
 Research Positions Open 
Research positions are available in the fields of biological wastewater
treatment, advanced oxidation processes for water and wastewater treatment,
nature-based solutions, and micropollutant/transformation product analysis.
Please email your CV to   monikadubey@iitj.ac.in

 People 
 
 
 P Ravi Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 P Ravi Prakash 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            P Ravi Prakash
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        rp[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Computational Mechanics; Structural Fire Engineering; Application of AI in Structural Engineering, Building Information Modelling (BIM) 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1661 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. P. Ravi Prakash   is an Assistant Professor in the  Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur  and an  Affiliate Faculty  at the  School of Artificial Intelligence and Data Sciences . His research focuses on  structural fire engineering, computational mechanics, and building information modeling (BIM). 
 His research group works on  fire-induced progressive collapse, discontinuum modeling of materials and structures, BIM-driven sustainable building design, and virtual twins. 
 Educational Qualifications 
 
 Ph.D.  – Indian Institute of Technology Gandhinagar (2018) 
 B.Tech.  – Acharya Nagarjuna University (2013) 
 
 Work Experience 
 
 Visiting Researcher  – Michigan State University, USA (September 2023 – February 2024) 
 Assistant Professor  – Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur (March 2021 – Present) 
 Assistant Professor  – National Institute of Technology Warangal (January 2020 – February 2021) 
 Postdoctoral Fellow  – Universidade do Minho, Portugal (December 2018 – December 2019) 
 
 Research Grants & Awards 
 Dr. Prakash has been awarded the  Extramural Research Grant  from the  DRDO, Ministry of Defence, Government of India (Armaments Research Board)  and the  Start-up Research Grant  from the  Anusandhan National Research Foundation (ANRF).  He is also a recipient of the prestigious  SIRE Fellowship  from ANRF. 
 Key Publications 
 His research has been published in leading journals, including  Engineering Fracture Mechanics, Fire Safety Journal, Computers & Structures,  and  Engineering Structures. 
 His expertise continues to contribute to advancements in  fire safety, computational modeling, and sustainable infrastructure design.

 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Tekcham Gishan Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 Tekcham Gishan Singh 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Tekcham Gishan Singh
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        tekcham[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Steel structure testing and design, high-strength steel, tubular and thin-walled structures, concrete-filled steel tubes, fire-resistant structural design, steel corrosion and retrofitting, sustainable infrastructure. 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1660 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About: 
                     
 
 
 Dr. Tekcham Gishan Singh  is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at the Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur. His research focuses on  structural testing, numerical modelling, and the design of steel structures. His broader research interests include  high-strength steel, tubular structures, concrete-filled steel tubes, fire-resistant structural design, corrosion, retrofitting of steel structures, hybrid structural systems, fatigue and fracture mechanics, and sustainable infrastructure .  His expertise contributes to advancements in structural engineering and the development of fire-resistant and earthquake-resilient infrastructure, ultimately aiding in the creation of safe and reliable design methods for steel structures. Dr. Singh earned his Ph.D. from the  Indian Institute of Technology Guwahati  in 2019 and completed his B.Tech. from the  National Institute of Technology Karnataka  in 2013. Before joining IIT Jodhpur in 2021, he worked as a Postdoctoral Researcher at  The Hong Kong Polytechnic University  from 2019 to 2021. He also gained industry experience as an Assistant Engineer at  Bangalore Metro Rail Corporation Ltd.  from 2013 to 2014. As part of his professional engagements, Dr. Singh serves as an  Editorial Board Member for  Scientific Reports , a journal published by the Nature Portfolio.

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Yogiraj Sargam 
 
 
 
 
 
 Yogiraj Sargam 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Yogiraj Sargam
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 Ph.D. Iowa State University, USA 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        ysargam[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Carbon Capture, Utilization, and Storage (CCUS), Waste valorization, Nanomaterials, Cement-based materials 
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1674 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About
                     
 
 
 Dr. Yogiraj Sargam is an Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering at IIT Jodhpur. He works in the research areas of carbon capture, utilization, and storage (CCUS), waste valorization, and nanomaterials within the cement and concrete value chain. Dr. Sargam earned his Ph.D. (2021) and M.S. (2019) from Iowa State University, USA, under the supervision of Prof. Kejin Wang. His doctoral work on the dispersion and stability of nanomaterials in cementitious systems earned him the Research Excellence Award from the university. His master’s research was focused on the measurement and prediction of mass concrete parameters and thermal conductivity. After his graduate studies, Dr. Sargam worked at CarbonCure Technologies, Canada (2021–2025), where he held roles of Research Scientist, Director of Research, and Senior Director of Research. During his tenure, he played a key role in advancing the understanding of CO2 mineralization mechanisms and developing CO2 utilization technologies for low-carbon concrete. Dr. Sargam has published extensively in high-impact journals, including Cement and Concrete Research, Journal of CO₂ Utilization, and Composites Part B, and actively collaborates with renowned researchers worldwide. His detailed research profile is available on  Google Scholar . If you are interested in joining Dr. Sargam’s research group or would like to collaborate with him, please reach out to him at  ysargam@iitj.ac.in .

 Civil and Infrastructure Engineering 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 Home 
 
 
 
 
 People 
 
 
 Sreejith Krishnan 
 
 
 
 
 
 Sreejith Krishnan 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                            Sreejith Krishnan
                         
 
                                Assistant Professor
                             
 
 
 
 
 
 school 
 
 PhD. Indian Institute of Technology Delhi 
 
 
 
 email 
 
 
 
                                        skrishnan[at]iitj[dot]ac[dot]in
                                     
 
 
 
 
 biotech 
 
 Material Characterization; Cement Chemistry; Concrete Technology; Durability and Repair of Structures;  
 
 
 
 call 
 
 0291 280 1670 
 
 
 
 language 
 
 
 Web Profile 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
                        About:
                     
 
 
 Dr. Sreejith Krishnan  is an  Assistant Professor in the Department of Civil and Infrastructure Engineering  at the  Indian Institute of Technology Jodhpur . His research focuses on the  chemistry of cementitious materials , with a particular interest in  low-carbon cement development, hydration and microstructure of alternative binders, and sustainable construction materials . Before joining IIT Jodhpur, Dr. Krishnan was a  Research Fellow at the University of Leeds, UK (2021–2023) , where he worked on the  MUSE project  under  Prof. Susan Bernal , developing novel  low-carbon cement from activated clays . Prior to that, he was a  Postdoctoral Researcher at EPFL, Switzerland (2019–2020) , working with  Prof. Karen Scrivener  on the  chemistry of belite in Portland cement  through a  Swiss Government Excellence Postdoctoral Fellowship . He obtained his  Ph.D. in Civil Engineering from IIT Delhi (2019)  under the supervision of  Prof. Shashank Bishnoi , as part of the  Low Carbon Cement Project , funded by the  Swiss Agency for Development and Cooperation . His research focused on  hydration and microstructure development in limestone calcined clay cements (LC3) and optimizing LC3 blend compositions . He also holds an  M.Tech. in Construction Engineering and Management from IIT Delhi (2014)  and a  B.Tech. in Civil Engineering from NIT Calicut (2012) . Dr. Krishnan has been recognized for his contributions to cement chemistry and sustainable construction. He received the  Distinction in Doctoral Research Award (IIT Delhi, 2019) , the  Swiss Government Excellence Fellowship (2019) , the  Advances in Cement Research Prize from the Institute of Civil Engineers, UK (2020) , and the  Prime Minister’s Early Career Research Grant from ANRF, India (2025) . 
 His expertise continues to drive advancements in  sustainable construction materials and the development of alternative cementitious systems .
